summary: Append-only domain event log over a part-owned Postgres table, written and queried through a contract-stable interface and a driver-free SqlExecutor seam.

exports: AuditLog { append(event: AuditEventInput): Promise<AuditEvent>; query(filter?: AuditQuery): Promise<AuditEvent[]> }
class AuditError extends Error { code: AuditErrorCode }

invariants: Importing the part performs no I/O and never throws; inputs are validated at call time, and every failure — invalid input or a storage error from the executor — surfaces as a typed AuditError (raw driver errors never escape)
append inserts exactly one row with a server-assigned monotonic id and occurred_at timestamp, returns the stored event, and the event is immediately readable via query
The log is append-only: no mutation is exported, and the database itself rejects UPDATE and DELETE on the events table (trigger) — the trail cannot be rewritten even with direct table access
query returns events newest-first by id, honors actor/action/target/since/until filters, and paginates deterministically via the `before` cursor under a bounded limit
action, target, and metadata round-trip faithfully; every value is parameterized, so SQL metacharacters in inputs are stored literally and never executed
An invalid event (blank action, over-long field, oversized metadata) or invalid query (non-positive or over-max limit) fails with a typed AuditError and issues zero SQL
The part operates solely through the provided SqlExecutor seam — it imports no database driver — and every statement it issues targets only its own audit_events table
notes: First DB-backed capability. The connection is an app-provided seam (the SqlExecutor shape `partkit migrate` already uses), not env or a vendored driver — so the part ships zero adapters and zero env, owns the `audit_events` table, and runs on the app's connection/transaction. occurred_at is server-assigned and not caller-settable: a trustworthy timeline is the defining property of an audit log. Conformance runs against real Postgres (gated on PARTKIT_TEST_DATABASE_URL); the input-validation and typed-error invariants also run DB-free. The suite lives with the implementing part until a second provider exists, then moves here (docs/02 §3-4).
